source: RecWalk- Nearly Uncoupled Random Walks for Top-N Recommendation

concepts.finding: RecWalk: random walks on user-item bipartite graph for top-N recommendation. Novel transition matrix design ensures nearly uncoupled Markov chain where walks stay within user or item communities before transitioning. Personalized recommendation as stationary distribution of random walk with restart. Outperforms item-based CF and MostPopular baselines.
concepts.implication: Random walk on keyword-intent bipartite graph for keyword-intent relatedness. Personalized PageRank from keyword seeds relevant intents. Nearly uncoupled design: walks explore keyword community before transitioning to intent community, modeling within-group similarity.
concepts.open_question: Is random-walk-based (PageRank) keyword-intent scoring more effective than embedding dot product for sparse keyword-intent graphs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: RecWalk-style random walk on keyword-intent graph produces higher recall for sparse keyword-intent relationships than embedding-based dot product retrieval.
